event: paravertebral painful muscular contracture of lumbar spine
no neurological deficit of lower extremities
lumbar MRI epidural abscess posteriorly of thecal sac at L3-L4 and L4-L5 levels
lumbar MRI epidural abscess anteriorly at L5-S1 level
decompressive laminectomy from L3 to S1
intravenous amoxicillin-clavulanic acid 2.2 g three times per day
blood cultures positive for Streptococcus mitis/oralis
ligamentum flavum fragment no relevant findings
BARD 5f picc-line placed in left basilic vein
empiric antibiotic treatment replaced by penicillin-G 5M units four times per day
minimum inhibitory concentration 0.125 mg/L for penicillin
CRP decreased from 256 mg/L at postoperative day 3 to 47 mg/L
antibiotic treatment changed to ceftriaxone 2 g daily for 4 weeks
lumbar MRI at 6-week follow-up showed L5-S1 collapse
no persistent or recurrent infection at 6-week follow-up
lumbar MRI at 1-year follow-up no infection
